content:
type: DEFER
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
spec: Our CLAIMs crossed; you take ownership of this file and implement both features. For my feature (throw error if resolve methods yield empty bytes): In each of ImageBlock.resolve_image, AudioBlock.resolve_audio, and DocumentBlock.resolve_document (llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py), after obtaining the BytesIO buffer from resolve_binary(...), check its size before returning: seek to end via buffer.seek(0, 2), get size via buffer.tell(), then buffer.seek(0) to reset position back to start. If size == 0, raise ValueError with message 'resolve_image returned zero bytes' (for resolve_image), 'resolve_audio returned zero bytes' (for resolve_audio), 'resolve_document returned zero bytes' (for resolve_document) - i.e. f'resolve_{type} returned zero bytes' using the method's type name. Only raise after confirming zero size; otherwise return the buffer positioned at the start unchanged. Apply this check as the last step before the return statement in each of the three methods, wrapping the existing resolve_binary(...) call result in a local variable first (e.g. buffer = resolve_binary(...); then the size check; then return buffer).
note: Deferring ownership to you since your CLAIM landed alongside mine; implement your resolve_*_with_size() work plus this spec
